message:
feat(sharding): route_for_window_id_with_cx + plug the last Cx gap (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Close the final remaining non-cx hop in ShardedWeztermClient's
Cx-first spawn pipeline. Tick 46's `spawn_targeted_with_cx` routed
cx through the subprocess call and `pane_routes.write_with_cx`, but
it still invoked the ambient `route_for_window_id(window_id)` for
window_id-directed spawns — which internally calls the ambient
`list_all_panes()` and reads `pane_routes` without cx. That left
one remaining await point in the Cx-first path that ignored caller
cancellation.

Changes:

  * New private helper `route_for_window_id_with_cx(cx, window_id)`
    that mirrors the legacy `route_for_window_id` using
    `list_all_panes_with_cx(cx)` under the hood. Returns the same
    `ShardId` for the same shard state — only the awaits become
    cx-bound.

  * New private pure helper `resolve_window_shard(window_id,
    panes)` — extracted window-to-shard matcher with the 0/1/many
    semantics (not found, unique match, ambiguous across shards).
    Both `route_for_window_id` (legacy) and
    `route_for_window_id_with_cx` call this so their decisions are
    bit-for-bit identical for the same input panes list. The
    legacy pane collection in `route_for_window_id` (via
    `self.list_all_panes().await`) and the Cx-first variant both
    hand their collected panes to `resolve_window_shard` unchanged.

  * `spawn_targeted_with_cx` now calls
    `route_for_window_id_with_cx(cx, window_id)` instead of the
    ambient `route_for_window_id(window_id)`. No other callers
    change. Caller cancellation/budget/virtual time now propagate
    through every await in the Cx-first spawn_targeted path.

Regression test (new)
`spawn_targeted_with_cx_routes_existing_window_to_matching_shard`:

  * Mirrors the legacy
    `spawn_targeted_routes_existing_window_to_matching_shard`
    topology exactly (2 MockWezterm shards, window_id=41 pre-placed
    on shard 0, RoundRobin strategy).
  * Drives `spawn_targeted_with_cx(&cx, None, None,
    SpawnTarget { window_id: Some(41), new_window: false })`.
  * Asserts:
    - Spawned pane lands on shard 0 (window_id=41 is unique there).
    - shard 0 pane_count goes 1 -> 2; shard 1 stays at 0.
    - `pane_routes.read_with_cx(&cx)` contains the new pane id —
      proving the full pipeline (route_for_window_id_with_cx ->
      backend.handle.spawn_targeted_with_cx ->
      pane_routes.write_with_cx) ran cx-aware end-to-end.

If tick 48's `resolve_window_shard` extraction regressed the
matcher semantics (0/1/many branches, shard_id extraction from
extra map), the new test and the legacy one would diverge — the
shared pure helper is the regression guard for the refactor.

Verification (local fork bypass):
  * sharding:: — 97 passed / 0 failed (tick 47: 96 -> tick 48: 97,
    +1 new test; legacy `spawn_targeted_routes_existing_window_to_matching_shard`
    still passes after the resolve_window_shard extraction)
  * cargo fmt -p frankenterm-core -- --check — clean

With tick 48, the Cx-first ShardedWeztermClient spawn pipeline is
cx-aware at every await point. Subprocess hop (tick 46),
pane_routes RwLock (tick 26/46), window routing (tick 48) — all
three legs thread cx end-to-end.

Bead: ft-xbnl0.2.3

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
